commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d15a0ca3fb527b5e6930514aafd8e6cc86aa9d52)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Release CLI workflow so manual dispatches upload workflow artifacts only, release asset uploads and Homebrew tap updates run only on release events, and release tag values are validated and passed through environment variables.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the manual tag publish path, and the PR regression is source-reproducible because RELEASE_TAG=main fails the new tag regex before artifact upload.
commands.review_followup: The contributor needs to fix the no-tag manual artifact regression and provide final-head workflow proof; release owners still need to approve the manual publishing policy.

target.title: Harden Release CLI manual dispatch
target.body:
Fixes #1153.

## Summary

- make manual `Release CLI` dispatches artifact-only, even when `tag` is provided
- keep GitHub release asset uploads limited to `release` events
- keep the Homebrew tap update limited to `release` events
- validate release tag values before packaging or tap dispatch
- pass the release tag and tap request id through environment variables instead of interpolating them into Bash source
- clarify that the manual `tag` input packages artifacts but does not publish them

## Why

The current workflow lets a manual dispatch build from the selected workflow ref while using `inputs.tag` as `RELEASE_TAG`. Because the upload step runs when `inputs.tag != ''` and uses `gh release upload "$TAG" ... --clobber`, a manual run can publish assets for a release tag from a different checkout ref.

ClawSweeper validated #1153 as source-repro, P1, impact:security, and fix-shape-clear. It also noted that a live repro against the real repo should not be run because it would mutate release assets.

This draft chooses the narrowest policy fix ClawSweeper suggested: manual runs build workflow artifacts only; publishing remains tied to release events.

A follow-up audit found one adjacent shell-hardening issue in the same workflow: release tag strings were still interpolated into Bash source in the tap-update job. This update folds that hardening into the same PR by validating tag-like values and moving the release tag/request id into step environment variables before shell use.

## Proof

Issue proof:

- #1153 has ClawSweeper `clawsweeper:source-repro`, `P1`, `impact:security`, `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`, and `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`.
- ClawSweeper's review says source inspection gives a high-confidence path: manual dispatch supplies `inputs.tag`, checkout remains on the workflow ref, and upload publishes to that tag with `--clobber`.

Patch proof:

```text
$ ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-cli.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'
yaml ok

$ rg -n "if: github.event_name == 'release'$|if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'$" .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
227:        if: github.event_name == 'release'
239:        if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
250:    if: github.event_name == 'release'

$ rg -n "inputs\\.tag != ''|github\\.event_name != 'release' && inputs\\.tag == ''" .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
# no matches

$ rg -n "tag=\"\\$\\{\\{ inputs\\.tag|steps\\.release\\.outputs\\.(tag|request_id)" .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
274:          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ steps.release.outputs.tag }}
275:          REQUEST_ID: ${{ steps.release.outputs.request_id }}
303:          REQUEST_ID: ${{ steps.release.outputs.request_id }}

$ for tag in v0.24 v0.29.2 v1.2.3-beta v1.2.3-beta.1 v-proof-1154 'v1.2$(echo bad)' 'release-1.2' 'v1/evil' 'v1 evil' ''; do if [[ -n "$tag" && "$tag" =~ ^v[0-9A-Za-z._-]+$ ]]; then printf 'ok %q\n' "$tag"; else printf 'reject %q\n' "$tag"; fi; done
ok v0.24
ok v0.29.2
ok v1.2.3-beta
ok v1.2.3-beta.1
ok v-proof-1154
reject v1.2\$\(echo\ bad\)
reject release-1.2
reject v1/evil
reject v1\ evil
reject ''

$ REQUEST_ID=codexbar-test gh api repos/steipete/CodexBar --jq 'env.REQUEST_ID'
codexbar-test

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Live fork proof:

I previously manually dispatched the patched `Release CLI` workflow on my fork with a harmless proof tag before the tag-validation follow-up:

```text
$ gh workflow run release-cli.yml --repo jskoiz/CodexBar --ref fix/release-cli-tag-provenance -f tag=v-proof-1154
https://github.com/jskoiz/CodexBar/actions/runs/26432040743
```

Run result:

- event: `workflow_dispatch`
- branch: `fix/release-cli-tag-provenance`
- commit: `45e2ad1dd5b16036e6b088de49970cb00a291c91`
- conclusion: `success`
- `update-homebrew-tap`: `skipped`

Relevant job-step results from that run:

```text
macos-x86_64: Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
linux-x64:    Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
linux-arm64:  Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
macos-arm64:  Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
```

Artifacts were uploaded for all four targets:

```text
codexbar-cli-macos-x86_64
codexbar-cli-linux-x64
codexbar-cli-linux-arm64
codexbar-cli-macos-arm64
```

One downloaded artifact confirmed the manual tag was packaged as an artifact rather than published as a release asset:

```text
/tmp/codexbar-proof-artifacts/CodexBarCLI-v-proof-1154-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
/tmp/codexbar-proof-artifacts/CodexBarCLI-v-proof-1154-linux-x86_64.tar.gz.sha256
```

I did not rerun the workflow after the tag-validation follow-up to avoid spending macOS runner minutes; the follow-up is covered by the local YAML, grep, regex, and `gh --jq env.REQUEST_ID` checks above. Both commits use `[skip ci]` to avoid implicit macOS CI on push/PR update. ClawSweeper may still rerun on the PR update.

If maintainers prefer preserving a manual repair-publish path, the alternate shape is to check out the requested tag, verify `HEAD` matches that tag before upload, validate the tag before any shell use, and avoid `--clobber` by default.
target.author: jskoiz

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: jskoiz
commands.command: re-review\n\nadded live fork workflow proof from https://github.com/jskoiz/codexbar/actions/runs/26432040743 showing workflow_dispatch with tag=v-proof-1154 skipped release upload and homebrew tap dispatch while uploading workflow artifacts for all four targets.
commands.freeform_prompt: re-review\n\nAdded live fork workflow proof from https://github.com/jskoiz/CodexBar/actions/runs/26432040743 showing workflow_dispatch with tag=v-proof-1154 skipped release upload and Homebrew tap dispatch while uploading workflow artifacts for all four targets.
